title: polystichum anomalum f. travancoricum
intro: Polystichum anomalum f. travancoricum, a lesser-known variant of the Polystichum anomalum fern species, is a type of evergreen fern native to the Western Ghats mountain range in India. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Polystichum anomalum f. travancoricum are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
